meta.setting: knife-edge ridge trail above creek and mining road country
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in hazardous horseback travel

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Rugged terrain and road movement through mining country.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Mountain Lion
research_grounding.use: Cougar sign and rough-country predator presence.

image_prompt.subject: a pack string inching along a knife-edge shale trail with a rider leading his horse and cougar tracks in the dust near the foreground
image_prompt.scene: windy high ridge before a storm; one mule balks at a tight turn, loose rope hangs from a saddle load, creek country drops far below, no signs
image_prompt.composition: square vertical-danger scene with trail cutting across the frame, drop visible below, storm sky and scrub-covered slope enclosing the route
image_prompt.camera: third-person over-trail view from behind the pack string, low enough to show footing and the drop
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the viewer feel the careful pause before the next hoof comes down.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine mountain trail scene
render_traits: slightly simplified riders, pack animals, brush, and ledge geometry
modest texture resolution on shale, tack, rope, cloth, scrub, and storm clouds
hard ambient occlusion under hooves, saddle loads, ledges, and brush roots
windy pre-storm light with dusty air
believable 1899 riding gear and pack loads
playable traversal composition with clear risk and no cinematic polish
style_modifiers.palette: shale gray, dust tan, horse brown, rope straw, scrub green, storm blue, and pale cliff gold.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable markings, signs, labels, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos
flat easy road with no drop

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The knife-edge trail, pack animals, rider, loose load, cougar tracks, drop, and storm are readable.
Every object belongs plausibly to rugged 1899 trail travel.
The image supports travel hazard, ambush, wildlife, and route-choice scenes.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
